desires: Get teammate_A to formally acknowledge the timeline extension today so I don't have to carry the burden of uncertainty that threatens our bond.
Secure a solid rest block tonight before tomorrow's cohort_3 meal, because my sleep debt and current stress levels are already making me feel like a liability to the group.
Avoid letting the deadline pressure override the renegotiation with teammate_A, even though it feels risky given my procrastination bias.

goals.description: Initiate a direct, assertive conversation with teammate_A to formally renegotiate the project timeline today, explicitly stating my capacity boundaries while prioritizing our group's trust over my personal schedule.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value (0.5) dictates that preserving the relationship and my reputation within cohort_3 is paramount. If I do not address this directly now, I risk appearing unreliable, which would damage the trust we've built. My assertive style requires me to voice these concerns clearly rather than silently absorbing the burden, even if it means delaying my own hard deadlines for the sake of the team dynamic. The afternoon has slipped by; waiting longer only increases the pressure on the group, making my negotiation harder.

goals.description: Secure a solid rest block tonight before tomorrow's cohort_3 meal, explicitly deferring the trivial easy chore since my energy reserves are critically low due to existing sleep debt.
goals.rationale: While I value health and need to manage my sleep debt, completing this task now feels like a distraction from the critical relational work at hand. However, given my low energy state, I will defer this until after the high-stakes conversation with teammate_A is resolved. Once the immediate tension is lowered and I have secured some relief through the dialogue, I can revisit this small task without feeling guilty or rushed. Protecting my long-term capacity is more important than an instant win right now.
